policy.purpose: Keep AI-facing Vais instructions synchronized with the compiler-facing language contract and public evidence boundary.
policy.rule: When language, compiler, playground, DB, server, or web behavior changes, update this registry first and regenerate the AI docs.

referenceApp.name: AI docs notes service
referenceApp.description: Small canonical Vais program that a new AI agent can reconstruct from docs/ai and docs/LANGUAGE_SPEC.md without relying on model memory.

changeTriggers.reason: Language or compiler behavior may have changed.
Public claims or AI-visible onboarding may have changed.
Package, server, database, or playground behavior may have changed.

features.title: Canonical syntax and compact controls
features.summary: Use full-word declarations where available and compact control forms where they remain the current language surface.
aiInstructions: Use fn, struct, enum, trait, impl, type, use, and pub in new examples.
Use I, L, LF, LW, B, C, D, A, Y, N, G, and O only for the compact forms listed in the language spec.
Do not rewrite Vais as Rust, TypeScript, C, or pseudocode.
mustUse: fn add(a: i64, b: i64) -> i64 = a + b
struct Point { x: f64, y: f64 }
avoid: Legacy F, S, EN declaration spellings in public examples
Invented Rust-style let statements
Unspecified keyword aliases

features.title: Strict type conversion boundaries
features.summary: Numeric adaptation is allowed in numeric contexts, but bool, str, structural types, and typed pointers do not silently become i64.
aiInstructions: Use explicit casts or helper APIs at pointer, bool, str, and raw address boundaries.
Preserve *T and i64 as different surfaces unless the language spec names a raw memory API.
Treat type-checking errors as source bugs, not compiler warnings to work around.
mustUse: typed_ptr as i64 only at an explicit raw interop boundary
avoid: bad_bool: i64 = true
bad_str: i64 = "hello"
Implicit *T to i64 unification

features.title: Generic enum layout contract
features.summary: Option<T> and Result<T, E> use the canonical erased runtime enum layout with an i32 tag and payload storage.
aiInstructions: Use Option<T> and Result<T, E> directly in APIs instead of inventing nullable or tuple encodings.
Assume match, constructors, calls, try lowering, and unwrap lowering share the same enum layout.
Do not claim per-instantiation named LLVM structs unless the language spec is changed first.
mustUse: match opt { Some(v) => v, None => fallback }
avoid: Anonymous enum literals such as { i8, i64 }
Mixing caller and callee enum layouts
Assuming a generic enum tag width other than i32

features.title: Result and Option control flow
features.summary: Result<T, E>, Option<T>, match, the try operator, and unwrap-style lowering are explicit language surfaces.
aiInstructions: Use Result<T, E> for recoverable failures and Option<T> for absence.
Use match when all handled variants must be visible to the reader.
Use ? only inside the A2-01/A2-02 Core-typed Result or Option predicates.
Use typed statement bindings such as x: i64 = fallible()? for try propagation.
Do not use ? as cleanup, ownership transfer, or resource-release proof.
mustUse: fn checked_div(a: i64, b: i64) -> Result<i64, str>
avoid: Throw/catch syntax
Null values
Implicit failure returns
? in functions that do not return matching Result or Option
Relying on ? to free DB/runtime resources

features.title: A2 certified subset boundaries
features.summary: The try operator, dyn dispatch, inline closures, and function-pointer parameters are certified only inside the predicates in A2_SUBSETS.md.
aiInstructions: Use ? only for Core-typed Result/Option value and error propagation in matching return functions.
Use dyn dispatch only for declared traits with visible impls; invalid non-implementers are source errors.
Use closures only as inline or synchronously invoked no-escape values.
Use function-pointer parameters only with named functions and Core-typed signatures.
mustUse: fn call(g: dyn Greet) -> i64 { g.greet() }
fn apply(f: fn(i64) -> i64, x: i64) -> i64
avoid: Using ? to clean up resources or transfer ownership
as dyn casts or passing values without a visible impl
Returning or storing closures that capture local variables
Stored function values or closure-to-fn-pointer coercions

features.title: References, raw pointers, and slices
features.summary: References, raw pointers, and slices are distinct type surfaces with explicit conversions and explicit dereference expectations.
aiInstructions: Use &T and &mut T for references, *T for typed raw pointers, and &[T] or &mut [T] for slices.
Use explicit dereference when a value is required from a reference.
Do not rely on automatic reference-to-value coercion.
Treat field access through a reference as different from implicit &T to T value conversion.
avoid: Implicit ref-to-value unification
Implicit typed pointer to raw address conversion
Treating slices as plain pointers without length

features.title: Explicit resource cleanup
features.summary: DB/runtime cleanup is explicit and manual unless a named gate promotes a narrower Drop behavior.
aiInstructions: Call the explicit free, close, unpin, or unlock helper on success and error paths.
Use EmbeddedResult.free_owned_text() for owned DB query result text.
Use Statement.free_parser_owned_text() and token cleanup helpers for parser-owned text.
Do not rely on automatic Drop, RAII, affine/linear annotations, or ? to release resources.
avoid: Automatic Token, Statement, or EmbeddedResult Drop
Broad Result cleanup on return or ? early return
Using affine or linear annotations as ownership enforcement
Leaving cleanup to panic hooks or runtime recovery

features.title: Rejected and controlled surface boundary
features.summary: A4, Rejected, and Controlled inventories are not supported coding surfaces unless the language spec or A2 subset docs name a narrower certified subset.
aiInstructions: Treat docs/certification/EXCLUDED_FEATURES.md as the source for A4, Controlled, Rejected, and Untested boundaries.
Use the explicit certified subset from docs/LANGUAGE_SPEC.md or docs/certification/A2_SUBSETS.md before writing code near a controlled coercion.
When a rejected surface fails with E001 or P001, change the source code instead of adding runtime fallback or assuming legacy opt-out environment flags.
Treat affine and linear annotations as type-carrier syntax only; do not claim move-only or use-count enforcement.
mustUse: x: i64 = fallible()? inside a matching Result or Option function
explicit *r when a value is needed from a reference
manual cleanup helpers for Token, Statement, and EmbeddedResult payloads
avoid: Integer truthy conditions such as I n { ... }
Implicit Box<T> to T, &T to T, Vec<T> to unrelated references, pointer to integer, or pointer to slice coercions
Struct literals with missing fields
? in a function that does not return matching Result or Option
Returned or stored closures that capture local variables
Linear or affine annotations as ownership enforcement

features.title: Playground mode contract
features.summary: The playground exposes named modes. Server-WASM is API compiled, Browser-JS is a smoke path, and preview mode is not certified compilation.
aiInstructions: Name Server-WASM as server-backed or API-compiled.
Name Browser-JS as a smoke path unless a stronger gate is added.
Do not claim complete browser-only compilation or execution.
avoid: Plain WASM label without the server dependency
Claiming browser-only compile/execute as complete
Treating preview mode as certified

features.title: DB, server, and web evidence boundary
features.summary: DB, server, and web behavior is public only where named gates cover it. Do not describe the ecosystem as product-complete.
aiInstructions: Use PUBLIC_STATUS.md before making DB, server, or web claims.
Name exact gate counts when documenting ecosystem behavior.
Treat ungated DB/server/web behavior as workbench behavior.
avoid: Product-complete v1.0
Single full ecosystem runtime aggregate main gate unless PUBLIC_STATUS.md says it exists
Unscoped production-readiness claims

features.title: Diagnostic and CLI boundary
features.summary: Only selected compiler-facing check, build, and pre-execution run failures have stable W1-C diagnostic envelopes.
aiInstructions: Use --no-update-check and --timeout 0 for deterministic local diagnostic checks.
Treat error[E001] type mismatches as source errors to fix, not warnings.
Use focused empirical or vaisc tests before claiming a diagnostic envelope.
Keep DB runtime Err envelopes separate from compiler diagnostics.
avoid: Panic hook output as a stable diagnostic
Crash-report files as user-facing behavior
LLVM verifier, clang, or linker text as stable product errors
Timeout wording, shell stderr ordering, or production observability claims

features.title: Verification-first development
features.summary: AI-written Vais code must be checked against the compiler and named gates instead of relying on model memory.
aiInstructions: Read docs/ai/LLM_LANGUAGE_CARD.md before writing code.
Compile or run the smallest relevant gate after every behavior change.
When a syntax guess fails, update the source to match docs/LANGUAGE_SPEC.md instead of inventing fallback syntax.
avoid: Unchecked generated code
Silent TODOs for compiler failures
Claims without named verification commands
